title: thymus praecox subsp. polytrichus
intro: Thymus praecox subsp. polytrichus, also known as wild thyme or creeping thyme, is a low-growing, spreading evergreen subshrub native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. The health benefits of Thymus praecox subsp. polytrichus include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and boost the immune system. Thymus praecox subsp. polytrichus can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in cooking and aromatherapy.
